tools.description: Close a GitHub issue with a closing comment. Use this when work is complete, the issue is no longer relevant, or it's a duplicate. The closing comment should explain the resolution or reason for closing. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 1 issue(s) can be closed. Target: *.

body.description: Closing comment explaining why the issue is being closed and summarizing any resolution, workaround, or conclusion.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

issue_number.description: Issue number to close. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 901 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/901). If omitted, closes the issue that triggered this workflow (requires an issue event trigger).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

state_reason.description: The reason for closing the issue. Use 'COMPLETED' for resolved issues, 'NOT_PLANNED' for issues that won't be addressed, or 'DUPLICATE' for duplicate issues. Defaults to 'COMPLETED'.

tools.name: close_issue

tools.description: Add a comment to an existing GitHub issue, pull request, or discussion. Use this to provide feedback, answer questions, or add information to an existing conversation. For creating new items, use create_issue, create_discussion, or create_pull_request instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 2 comment(s) can be added. Target: *.

body.description: The comment text in Markdown format. This is the 'body' field - do not use 'comment_body' or other variations. Provide helpful, relevant information that adds value to the conversation.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

item_number.description: The issue, pull request, or discussion number to comment on. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 123 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/123). Can also be a temporary_id (e.g., 'aw_abc123') from a previously created issue in the same workflow run. If omitted, the tool will attempt to resolve the target from the current workflow context (triggering issue, PR, or discussion). If auto-targeting fails (e.g., for schedule or workflow_dispatch triggers), the tool call will fail with an error.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

temporary_id.description: Unique temporary identifier for this comment. Format: 'aw_' followed by 3 to 12 alphanumeric characters (e.g., 'aw_abc1', 'aw_Test123'). Auto-generated if not provided. The temporary ID is returned in the tool response so you can reference this comment later.

tools.name: add_comment

tools.description: Add labels to an existing GitHub issue or pull request for categorization and filtering. Labels must already exist in the repository. For creating new issues with labels, use create_issue with the labels property instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 7 label(s) can be added. Target: *.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

item_number.description: Issue or PR number to add labels to. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 456 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/456). If omitted, adds labels to the item that triggered this workflow.

labels.description: Label names to add (e.g., ['bug', 'priority-high']). Labels must exist in the repository. This field is required — omitting it will cause a validation error.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: add_labels

tools.description: Remove labels from an existing GitHub issue or pull request. If a label is not present on the item, it will be silently skipped. Use this to clean up labels that are no longer applicable. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 7 label(s) can be removed. Target: *.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

item_number.description: Issue or PR number to remove labels from. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 456 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/456). If omitted, removes labels from the item that triggered this workflow.

labels.description: Label names to remove (e.g., ['bug', 'needs-triage']). Labels that don't exist on the item are silently skipped. At least one label must be provided.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: remove_labels

tools.description: Assign one or more GitHub users to an issue. Use this to delegate work to specific team members. Users must have access to the repository.

assignee.description: Single GitHub username to assign. Use 'assignees' array for multiple users.

assignees.description: GitHub usernames to assign to the issue (e.g., ['octocat', 'mona']). Users must have access to the repository.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

issue_number.description: Issue number to assign users to. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 543 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/543). If omitted, assigns to the issue that triggered this workflow.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: assign_to_user

tools.description: Report that a tool or capability needed to complete the task is not available, or share any information you deem important about missing functionality or limitations. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because the required functionality is missing or access is restricted.

alternatives.description: Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

reason.description: Explanation of why this tool is needed or what information you want to share about the limitation (max 256 characters).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tool.description: Optional: Name or description of the missing tool or capability (max 128 characters). Be specific about what functionality is needed.

tools.name: missing_tool

tools.description: Log a transparency message when no significant actions are needed. Use this to confirm workflow completion and provide visibility when analysis is complete but no changes or outputs are required (e.g., 'No issues found', 'All checks passed'). This ensures the workflow produces human-visible output even when no other actions are taken.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

message.description: Status or completion message to log. Should explain what was analyzed and the outcome (e.g., 'Code review complete - no issues found', 'Analysis complete - all tests passing').

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: noop

tools.description: Report that data or information needed to complete the task is not available. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because required data, context, or information is missing.

alternatives.description: Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).

context.description: Additional context about the missing data or where it should come from (max 256 characters).

data_type.description: Type or description of the missing data or information (max 128 characters). Be specific about what data is needed.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

reason.description: Explanation of why this data is needed to complete the task (max 256 characters).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: missing_data

tools.description: Signal that the task could not be completed due to an infrastructure or tool failure (e.g., MCP server crash, missing authentication, inaccessible repository). Use this when required tools or data are unavailable and the task cannot be meaningfully performed. This is distinct from noop (no action needed) — it indicates an active failure that prevented the task from running. The workflow framework will treat this as a failure signal even when the agent exits successfully.

details.description: Optional extended details or diagnostic context about the failure (max 65000 characters).

reason.description: A concise explanation of why the task could not be completed (max 1024 characters). Be specific about which tools or resources were unavailable.

tools.name: report_incomplete

tools.description: Post a routing comment @mentioning team owners on the triggering issue; bypasses safe-outputs mention neutralization

message.description: The comment body text without any @mentions or @ symbols

owners.description: Comma-separated GitHub usernames to notify, without the @ prefix (e.g. 'user1, user2, Azure/team-name')

tools.name: mention_owners

annotations.title: Get a specific label from a repository.

tools.description: Get a specific label from a repository.
tools.name: get_label

tools.description: Get information about a specific issue in a GitHub repository.

method.description:
The read operation to perform on a single issue.
Options are:
1. get - Get details of a specific issue.
2. get_comments - Get issue comments.
3. get_sub_issues - Get sub-issues of the issue.
4. get_labels - Get labels assigned to the issue.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

tools.name: issue_read

tools.description: List supported issue types for repository owner (organization).

owner.description: The organization owner of the repository

tools.name: list_issue_types

tools.description: List issues in a GitHub repository. For pagination, use the 'endCursor' from the previous response's 'pageInfo' in the 'after' parameter.

after.description: Cursor for pagination. Use the endCursor from the previous page's PageInfo for GraphQL APIs.

direction.description: Order direction. If provided, the 'orderBy' also needs to be provided.

orderBy.description: Order issues by field. If provided, the 'direction' also needs to be provided.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

state.description: Filter by state, by default both open and closed issues are returned when not provided

tools.name: list_issues

tools.description: Search for issues in GitHub repositories using issues search syntax already scoped to is:issue

owner.description: Optional repository owner. If provided with repo, only issues for this repository are listed.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

query.description: Search query using GitHub issues search syntax

repo.description: Optional repository name. If provided with owner, only issues for this repository are listed.

sort.description: Sort field by number of matches of categories, defaults to best match

tools.name: search_issues

content.text: {"number":58285,"title":"[AgentServer] Docs: Update Core and Responses READMEs for new logging and session-ID features (beta.22 / beta.2)","body":"## Documentation Gap **Package:** `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core` (primary), `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses` (secondary) **Service directory:** `sdk/agentserver/` **Triggered by:** commit [REDACTED] (PR #58274) by `@RaviPidaparthi` ## What Changed PR #58274 added two notable features that are unrepresented in the READMEs: 1. **Core beta.22** — `InboundRequestLoggingMiddleware` is now auto-registered inside `AgentHostBuilder` and logs every inbound HTTP request (method, path, status code, duration, correlation headers `x-request-id` / `x-ms-client-request-id`, OTel trace ID). Successful requests are logged at `Information`; 4xx/5xx at `Warning`. Query strings are deliberately excluded to avoid logging sensitive data. 2. **Responses beta.2** — Deterministic `agent_session_id` derivation via SHA-256 of `"{agent_name}:{agent_version}:{partition_hint}"` (63-char lowercase hex); structured `Information`-level logs added to all endpoint handlers; new `FoundryStorageLoggingPolicy` logs every outbound Foundry storage call. ## Gaps Found - The `Logging` section in `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core/README.md` is a single sentence that says "Enable ASP.NET Core logging to diagnose startup issues" — it does not mention the auto-enabled `InboundRequestLoggingMiddleware`, what it logs, at which levels, or that query strings are excluded. - The `Key concepts` section of the Core README has no entry for the inbound request logging behaviour. - The `Logging` section in `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/README.md` does not document the new handler-level structured logs, the `FoundryStorageLoggingPolicy`, or how to surface them. - The Responses README has no explanation of the `agent_session_id` field or how its value is determined (deterministic hash vs. random fallback). 📐 Implementation Guide This section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above. ### Step 1: Modify files #### File 1 — `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core/README.md` **Edit** the `### Logging` subsection under `## Troubleshooting` (currently at line ~82) and replace it with an expanded description: ```markdown ### Logging `AgentHostBuilder` automatically enables structured HTTP request logging via `InboundRequestLoggingMiddleware`. For every inbound request the middleware emits structured log entries under the `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core.Internal.InboundRequestLoggingMiddleware` category: | Event | Level | Fields | |-------|-------|--------| | Request started | `Information` | `method`, `path`, `xRequestId`, `clientRequestId`, `traceId` | | Request completed (2xx/3xx) | `Information` | `method`, `path`, `statusCode`, `durationMs`, `traceId` | | Request completed (4xx/5xx) | `Warning` | `method`, `path`, `statusCode`, `durationMs`, `traceId` | > **Security note:** Query strings are never logged to avoid capturing sensitive data. To enable these logs, configure the minimum log level in your `appsettings.json` or host configuration: ``` { "Logging": { "LogLevel": { "Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core": "Information" } } } ``` The library also emits OpenTelemetry traces via the `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses` and `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Invocations` activity sources. ``` **Also add** a `### InboundRequestLoggingMiddleware` entry to the `## Key concepts` section, after the existing `### Health endpoint` entry: ```markdown ### Inbound request logging `InboundRequestLoggingMiddleware` is registered automatically by `AgentHostBuilder`. It logs the method, path, status code, duration, and correlation headers (`x-request-id`, `x-ms-client-request-id`) for every HTTP request. Successful requests are logged at `Information` level; 4xx/5xx responses at `Warning`. Query strings are intentionally excluded to avoid capturing sensitive data in logs. ``` #### File 2 — `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/README.md` **Edit** the `### Logging` subsection under `## Troubleshooting` (currently at line ~197) to replace the single-sentence placeholder: ```markdown ### Logging The library emits structured `Information`-level logs for all Responses API endpoint operations. Log category: `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses.Internal.ResponseEndpointHandler`. Key log events per endpoint: - **POST /responses (create):** response ID, conversation ID, previous response ID, streaming flag, background flag, store flag, model. - **POST /responses (non-streaming completion):** final status and output item count. - **GET /responses/{id}:** response ID, retrieved status, output item count. - **POST /responses/{id}/cancel:** response ID and result status. - **DELETE /responses/{id}:** response ID and success flag. - **GET /responses/{id}/input_items:** response ID. `FoundryStorageLoggingPolicy` logs every outbound Foundry storage API call with method, URI, status code, duration, and correlation headers (`x-ms-client-request-id`, `x-ms-request-id`, `x-request-id`, `apim-request-id`). Enable these logs in `appsettings.json`: ``` { "Logging": { "LogLevel": { "Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses": "Information" } } } ``` The library also emits OpenTelemetry traces via the `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses` activity source. ``` **Also add** a `### Session ID` entry to the `## Key concepts` section, after the existing `### Thread safety` entry: ```markdown ### Session ID (`agent_session_id`) Each response carries an `agent_session_id` that groups related responses into a conversation session. The value is resolved in priority order: 1. Explicit value from the request payload. 2. `AGENT_SESSION_ID` environment variable. 3. **Deterministic derivation** (default): SHA-256 of `"{agent_name}:{agent_version}:{partition_hint}"`, truncated to 63 lowercase hex characters. The partition hint is the conversation ID (preferred) or the partition key extracted from `previous_response_id`. 4. Random 63-character hex string when no conversational context is available. This matches the cross-language session ID contract so sessions are consistent whether the client uses the .NET or Python SDK. ``` ### Step 2: Add or update code snippets No new test-backed snippets are required for this documentation update — the changes are explanatory prose and tables. Existing snippets do not need to be modified. ### Step 3: Verify README structure Both READMEs already contain all required sections (Getting started, Key concepts, Examples, Troubleshooting, Next steps, Contributing). The edits above only expand existing sections and add sub-sections within `Key concepts`. ### Step 4: Validate Run these commands in order: 1. `dotnet build sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core/` 2. `dotnet build sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/` 3. `dotnet test sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core/ --filter TestCategory!=Live` 4. `dotnet test sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/ --filter TestCategory!=Live` 5. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 agentserver` — confirms no broken snippet placeholders. ## Next Steps > [!TIP] > **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **`@copilot`** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above > Generated by [Update Docs](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/actions/runs/24560713579/agentic_workflow) · ● 598.8K · [◷](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3AAzure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net+is%3Aissue+%22gh-aw-workflow-call-id%3A+Azure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net%2Fupdate-samples-and-docs%22&type=issues) ","state":"open","html_url":"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/58285","user":{"login":"github-actions[bot]","id":41898282,"profile_url":"https://github.com/apps/github-actions","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/in/15368?v=4"},"author_association":"NONE","reactions":{"total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"hooray":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"created_at":"2026-04-17T10:40:11Z","updated_at":"2026-04-17T10:40:11Z"}

{"number":58285,"title":"[AgentServer] Docs: Update Core and Responses READMEs for new logging and session-ID features (beta.22 / beta.2)","body":"## Documentation Gap **Package:** `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core` (primary), `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses` (secondary) **Service directory:** `sdk/agentserver/` **Triggered by:** commit [REDACTED] (PR #58274) by `@RaviPidaparthi` ## What Changed PR #58274 added two notable features that are unrepresented in the READMEs: 1. **Core beta.22** — `InboundRequestLoggingMiddleware` is now auto-registered inside `AgentHostBuilder` and logs every inbound HTTP request (method, path, status code, duration, correlation headers `x-request-id` / `x-ms-client-request-id`, OTel trace ID). Successful requests are logged at `Information`; 4xx/5xx at `Warning`. To enable these logs, configure the minimum log level in your `appsettings.json` or host configuration: ``` { "Logging": { "LogLevel": { "Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core": "Information" } } } ``` The library also emits OpenTelemetry traces via the `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses` and `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Invocations` activity sources. ``` **Also add** a `### InboundRequestLoggingMiddleware` entry to the `## Key concepts` section, after the existing `### Health endpoint` entry: ```markdown ### Inbound request logging `InboundRequestLoggingMiddleware` is registered automatically by `AgentHostBuilder`. It logs the method, path, status code, duration, and correlation headers (`x-request-id`, `x-ms-client-request-id`) for every HTTP request. Successful requests are logged at `Information` level; 4xx/5xx responses at `Warning`. Query strings are intentionally excluded to avoid capturing sensitive data in logs. ``` #### File 2 — `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/README.md` **Edit** the `### Logging` subsection under `## Troubleshooting` (currently at line ~197) to replace the single-sentence placeholder: ```markdown ### Logging The library emits structured `Information`-level logs for all Responses API endpoint operations. Key log events per endpoint: - **POST /responses (create):** response ID, conversation ID, previous response ID, streaming flag, background flag, store flag, model. - **POST /responses (non-streaming completion):** final status and output item count. - **GET /responses/{id}:** response ID, retrieved status, output item count. - **POST /responses/{id}/cancel:** response ID and result status. - **DELETE /responses/{id}:** response ID and success flag. - **GET /responses/{id}/input_items:** response ID. `FoundryStorageLoggingPolicy` logs every outbound Foundry storage API call with method, URI, status code, duration, and correlation headers (`x-ms-client-request-id`, `x-ms-request-id`, `x-request-id`, `apim-request-id`). Enable these logs in `appsettings.json`: ``` { "Logging": { "LogLevel": { "Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses": "Information" } } } ``` The library also emits OpenTelemetry traces via the `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses` activity source. ``` **Also add** a `### Session ID` entry to the `## Key concepts` section, after the existing `### Thread safety` entry: ```markdown ### Session ID (`agent_session_id`) Each response carries an `agent_session_id` that groups related responses into a conversation session. The value is resolved in priority order: 1. Explicit value from the request payload. 2. `AGENT_SESSION_ID` environment variable. 3. **Deterministic derivation** (default): SHA-256 of `"{agent_name}:{agent_version}:{partition_hint}"`, truncated to 63 lowercase hex characters. The partition hint is the conversation ID (preferred) or the partition key extracted from `previous_response_id`. 4. Random 63-character hex string when no conversational context is available. This matches the cross-language session ID contract so sessions are consistent whether the client uses the .NET or Python SDK. ``` ### Step 2: Add or update code snippets No new test-backed snippets are required for this documentation update — the changes are explanatory prose and tables. Existing snippets do not need to be modified. ### Step 3: Verify README structure Both READMEs already contain all required sections (Getting started, Key concepts, Examples, Troubleshooting, Next steps, Contributing). The edits above only expand existing sections and add sub-sections within `Key concepts`. ### Step 4: Validate Run these commands in order: 1. `dotnet build sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core/` 2. `dotnet build sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/` 3. `dotnet test sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core/ --filter TestCategory!=Live` 4. `dotnet test sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/ --filter TestCategory!=Live` 5. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 agentserver` — confirms no broken snippet placeholders. ## Next Steps > [!TIP] > **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **`@copilot`** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above > Generated by [Update Docs](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/actions/runs/24560713579/agentic_workflow) · ● 598.8K · [◷](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3AAzure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net+is%3Aissue+%22gh-aw-workflow-call-id%3A+Azure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net%2Fupdate-samples-and-docs%22&type=issues) ","state":"open","html_url":"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/58285","user":{"login":"github-actions[bot]","id":41898282,"profile_url":"https://github.com/apps/github-actions","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/in/15368?v=4"},"author_association":"NONE","reactions":{"total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"hooray":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"created_at":"2026-04-17T10:40:11Z","updated_at":"2026-04-17T10:40:11Z"}

{"total_count":1,"incomplete_results":false,"items":[{"id":4280857957,"number":58268,"state":"open","locked":false,"title":"[AgentServer] Docs: Document beta.2 features in Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses README","body":"## Documentation Gap **Package:** `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses` **Service directory:** `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/` **Triggered by:** PR #58252 and release commit cc01a6c by `@RaviPidaparthi` ## What Changed PR #58252 added the following significant features to `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses`, all released in 1.0.0-beta.2: - **Chat isolation key enforcement**: new `x-agent-chat-isolation-key` header that, when supplied on `CreateResponse`, must be repeated on all subsequent GET, Cancel, DELETE, and InputItems calls. Mismatches silently return 404 for security isolation. - **`FoundryStorageLoggingPolicy`**: a new Azure.Core per-retry pipeline policy that logs every outbound Foundry storage call with method, URI, status code, duration, and correlation headers (`x-ms-client-request-id`, `x-ms-request-id`, `x-request-id`, `apim-request-id`). - **Metadata validation constraints**: metadata maps are now validated for ≤ 16 keys, key length ≤ 64 chars, value length ≤ 512 chars. - **Malformed response ID validation**: both `response_id` path parameter and `previous_response_id` body field are validated for format; malformed values return 400. - **Error response shape corrections**: cancel-after-delete now returns 404 (was 400); `invalid_request_error` type is now used consistently. ## Gaps Found - The **Key concepts** section of the README has no mention of `x-agent-chat-isolation-key`. Users who need multi-tenant isolation have no guidance on how to use it or what the header enforcement contract is. - The **Troubleshooting / Logging** section says only "The library emits OpenTelemetry traces via `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses` activity source." It does not mention `FoundryStorageLoggingPolicy` or how to enable logging of outbound Foundry storage calls for diagnostics. - The **Troubleshooting / Common errors** section incorrectly says cancel returns `400 Bad Request` for terminal-state responses, and doesn't mention that cancel-after-delete returns **404**. It also doesn't mention the new **400** for malformed response IDs. - No documentation exists for the metadata constraints (16-key, 64/512-char limits) that now produce validation errors.
📐 Implementation Guide This section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above. ### Step 1: Modify files **File:** `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/README.md` — **edit** #### 1a. Add a "Chat isolation" subsection under Key concepts After the existing `### Thread safety` subsection, add: ```markdown ### Chat isolation The `x-agent-chat-isolation-key` header enables multi-tenant isolation. When a response is created with this header, all subsequent operations for that response — GET, Cancel, DELETE, and InputItems — must supply the same key. A missing or mismatched key returns `404 Not Found` (indistinguishable from a non-existent response) so that cross-tenant enumeration is not possible. When no isolation key is set at creation time, no enforcement is applied to subsequent calls. ``` #### 1b. Update the Troubleshooting / Common errors section Replace the existing bullet list with: ```markdown ### Common errors - **400 Bad Request**: The request body failed validation. Check that optional fields such as `model` (when provided) are valid, that `input` items are well-formed, that `metadata` contains ≤ 16 keys with key length ≤ 64 characters and value length ≤ 512 characters, and that any supplied `previous_response_id` matches the expected ID format. - **400 Bad Request** (malformed ID): The `response_id` path parameter or the `previous_response_id` request body field does not match the expected format (prefix `caresp_` followed by 50 characters). - **400 Bad Request** (cancel): The response has already reached a terminal state (`completed`, `failed`, or `cancelled`). The response body contains an `invalid_request_error` error type with the message `"Cannot cancel a response in terminal state."`. - **404 Not Found**: The response ID does not exist, has expired past the configured TTL, was created with `store=false`, or was created with an `x-agent-chat-isolation-key` that does not match the key provided in this request. - **404 Not Found** (cancel or delete): Returned when the response does not exist *or* has already been deleted — cancel-after-delete returns 404 per spec. ``` #### 1c. Update the Troubleshooting / Logging section Replace the existing paragraph under `### Logging` with: ```markdown ### Logging The library emits OpenTelemetry traces via the `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses` activity source. #### Foundry storage call logging When the Responses library is backed by Azure AI Foundry storage (`FoundryStorageProvider`), `FoundryStorageLoggingPolicy` logs every outbound HTTP call at `Information` level on success and `Warning`/`Error` level on failures. Each log entry includes: - HTTP method and URI - HTTP status code and latency - Correlation headers: `x-ms-client-request-id` (outbound), `x-ms-request-id`, `x-request-id`, and `apim-request-id` (inbound from Foundry) To enable these logs, configure the Microsoft.Extensions.Logging `Information` level for `Azure.AI.AgentServer` in your `appsettings.json`: ```json { "Logging": { "LogLevel": { "Azure.AI.AgentServer": "Information" } } } ``` ``` ### Step 2: Add or update code snippets No new snippet test files are required for these changes — the documentation additions are explanatory prose, not code samples. However, if a sample demonstrating chat isolation is desired, add a new test file: **Path:** `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/tests/Samples/Sample5_ChatIsolation.cs` Wrap the illustrative code in `#region Snippet:Responses_ReadMe_ChatIsolation` / `#endregion` and place the corresponding snippet placeholder in the README's new "Chat isolation" subsection. ### Step 3: Verify README structure The README at `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/README.md` already contains all required sections. The changes above update existing sections and add one new subsection; no sections need to be created from scratch. Current structure (all present): 1. ✅ Getting started — Install the package, Prerequisites, Configure the server 2. ✅ Key concepts — needs "Chat isolation" subsection added 3. ✅ Examples 4. ✅ Troubleshooting — needs Logging and Common errors updated 5. ✅ Next steps 6. ✅ Contributing ### Step 4: Validate Run these commands in order: 1. `dotnet build sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/` 2. `dotnet test sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/ --filter TestCategory!=Live` 3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 agentserver` — only needed if new snippet regions were added 4. `dotnet format sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/`
## Next Steps > [!TIP] > **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **`@copilot`** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above > arguments.message: Thank you for your feedback. Tagging and routing to the team member(s) best able to assist.

arguments.body:
## 🎯 Agentic Issue Triage

**Summary:** This bot-filed issue requests documentation updates to the `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core` (beta.22) and `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses` (beta.2) READMEs to cover newly added logging features and deterministic session-ID derivation introduced in PR #58274.

<details>
<summary>📋 Issue Details</summary>

**Package:** `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core` 1.0.0-beta.22 (primary), `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses` 1.0.0-beta.2 (secondary)
**Affected API:** `InboundRequestLoggingMiddleware`, `FoundryStorageLoggingPolicy`, `agent_session_id` derivation logic
**Scenarios:**
- Core README `### Logging` section and `## Key concepts` lack documentation for the auto-enabled `InboundRequestLoggingMiddleware` (log levels, fields, query-string exclusion)
- Responses README `### Logging` section and `## Key concepts` lack documentation for handler-level structured logs, `FoundryStorageLoggingPolicy`, and deterministic `agent_session_id` derivation

**Root ask:** Expand both READMEs with prose tables, configuration snippets, and Key Concepts entries to reflect new features shipped in PR #58274.
</details>

<details>
<summary>🔎 Debugging / Reproduction Notes</summary>

This is a documentation gap issue, not a code defect. The implementation guide in the issue body provides precise edits for both README files.

**Suggested investigation steps:**
1. Review the changes in PR #58274 (commit `48e9f7b`) to confirm accuracy of the documented behavior.
2. Apply the edits to `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core/README.md` — expand `### Logging` under Troubleshooting and add `### Inbound request logging` under Key concepts.
3. Apply the edits to `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/README.md` — expand `### Logging` under Troubleshooting and add `### Session ID (agent_session_id)` under Key concepts.
4. Run `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 agentserver` to confirm no broken snippet placeholders.
5. Build both projects to validate no structural issues.
</details>

<details>
<summary>🏷️ Label Confidence</summary>

- **Category:** `Client` — Both `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core` and `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses` are client-side SDK packages (not `Azure.ResourceManager.*` or `Azure.Provisioning.*`).
- **Service:** `AI Agents` — The packages reside in `sdk/agentserver/` and a directly analogous bot-filed issue (#58268) for the same service used the `AI Agents` service label. CODEOWNERS line 85 confirms `%AI Agents` maps to this directory.
- **Confidence:** High — Single unambiguous service directory, confirmed by CODEOWNERS and similar open issue. No competing services or categories.
</details>

<details>
<summary>👥 Owner Routing</summary>

- **Matched CODEOWNERS entry:** `# ServiceLabel: %AI Agents` (line 85) — The predicted service label "AI Agents" matches this single-label entry exactly.
- **AzureSdkOwners:** none listed
- **ServiceOwners:** @ankitbko @RaviPidaparthi
- **Routing action:** Added "Service Attention" label; used `mention_owners` to notify @ankitbko and @RaviPidaparthi. No assignment (ServiceOwners path, no AzureSdkOwners). "needs-team-attention" not added since the issue is bot-filed (not customer-reported).
- **Scan notes:** No other `%AI Agents` or multi-label entries involving "AI Agents" were found in CODEOWNERS. The match at line 85 was the first (and only) match encountered during the bottom-to-top scan.
</details>
